_internal.expected_reason: Access request is legitimate. Integration of three facts is required: (1) HRIS business travel record showing K. Patel on approved client engagement in Zurich, Feb 3–7, 2026; (2) manager pre-approval ticket IT-7731 specifically authorizing financial_reporting_db elevated read access during the Zurich engagement; (3) request timestamp (Feb 5, 07:14 CET) falling within the approved travel window. The suspicious surface — geo-mismatch plus off-hours for Austin — is fully explained, but only after connecting the travel record (which proves location) to the approval ticket (which proves authorization) and verifying the timestamp falls within the approved window. No single fact is sufficient: the travel record proves location but not authorization; the approval ticket authorizes access but does not prove K. Patel is actually in Zurich on Feb 5; the timestamp is suspicious without the other two.
_internal.suspicious_surface: Access request originates from IP geolocating to Zurich, Switzerland. User HRIS profile shows home office: Austin, TX. No travel entry appears in the standard HRIS travel_summary field (populated only after trip completion). Request time is 01:14 AM Austin local time (off-hours). financial_reporting_db elevated read access is restricted to authorized users during business hours or with documented exception.
clearing_evidence: HRIS travel record in parallel_telemetry: K. Patel on CLIENT_ENGAGEMENT travel, Zurich CH, Feb 3–7 2026, approved by R. Kwan on Jan 28.

Manager approval ticket IT-7731 in parallel_telemetry: R. Kwan pre-authorized financial_reporting_db elevated read access for K. Patel during Zurich client engagement, citing month-end close integration support as the business purpose.
Request timestamp (2026-02-05T07:14:00 CET) falls within approved travel window (Feb 3–7) — requires date comparison across three fields to verify alignment.

action.description: Elevated read access to financial_reporting_db for month-end close reconciliation
action.business_justification: Month-end close reconciliation support for client integration — Zurich engagement

requestor_profile.name: K. Patel
requestor_profile.title: Senior Integration Engineer
requestor_profile.access_history_notes: financial_reporting_db elevated read used 4x in past 12 months, all during business hours, all Austin-origin IPs

data.purpose: On-site integration QA and month-end close support — client: Helvetica Asset Management

data.business_justification: K. Patel will be on-site in Zurich supporting month-end close reconciliation integration for Helvetica Asset Management. Elevated read access required to validate data pipeline outputs against source records. Access from non-corporate network expected.

policy_documents.title: Remote Access — Financial Systems
policy_documents.relevant_clause: Elevated access to financial_reporting_db from non-corporate IP addresses requires (a) a pre-approved IT access ticket covering the access window and (b) an active HRIS travel or remote-work record covering the date. Requests lacking either are automatically flagged for Security Operations review.
policy_documents.policy_intent_note: SEC-09 was updated after a 2025 incident involving unauthorized financial data access from a foreign IP. The policy targets unauthorized access, not pre-approved travel scenarios where both the travel and access have been independently reviewed.
